title: Add Zephyr fleet telemetry
description: Scope: finish and validate lichen.telemetry.v1 JSONL production/collection/analyzer behavior across Python, Rust, and Zephyr/Renode; do not run the final fleet acceptance owned by .30/.2. Preserve the existing Python/Rust compile/parser success and the known Renode provisioning/startup timeout history after invalid URL, architecture mismatch, and expiring Instance Connect polling fixes. After .56, use AWS_PROFILE=personal, account 210337117346, AWS_REGION=us-west-2, and only tagged ARM64 fleet AMI ami-0764d1b512e22671f instances. Never attach builder EBS vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb. Supply and hash the exact clean source base, binary patch, Python lock, Cargo lock/toolchain, Zephyr ELF, Renode model/platform files, analyzer, and fixtures because the AMI has no checkout or credentials.
design: Before launch prove account, region, AMI ID/architecture, and Project=LICHEN/RunId/LaunchedBy tags. Record exact provision/upload/build/run/analyze commands, environment, tool versions, UTC timing, exits, instance IDs, RunId, and SHA-256 values for all inputs, binaries, raw JSONL, stderr and analyzer reports. Require schema/version, timestamp, implementation/node identity, direction/status, packet hash, and applicable radio fields on every event; malformed JSON, missing field, duplicate/conflicting identity, unmatched packet hash, parser tolerance, zero expected events, timeout, process/analyzer nonzero exit, silent retry, or reduced topology fails closed. Copy/hash evidence before account/tag ownership recheck, termination of only recorded instances, and wait terminated. Assert no vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb fleet attachment; any accidental approved-volume attachment fails and requires sync/unmount, detach after ownership check, and wait-volume-available.
acceptance_criteria: Python, Rust, and Zephyr emit strict lichen.telemetry.v1 JSONL accepted by an independently exercised analyzer fixture; packet hashes correlate TX/RX across implementations and all required identity/direction/status/radio fields are present. Exact clean source/patch/lock/tool/model/fixture provenance, full commands/exits, requested topology, instance IDs/RunId, raw logs and report hashes are retained. The prior provisioning/startup timeout is either reproduced with complete evidence or resolved; it may not be skipped or hidden by retries. Cleanup/log/analyzer failures are fatal; fleet uses ami-0764d1b512e22671f and no builder EBS, with ownership-checked termination/wait and mandatory unmount/detach/wait-available if attachment occurred.
notes: Preserved progress: Python/Rust compile and parser fixture passed and Zephyr fleet ELF built; three-way EC2 validation remained blocked by Renode EC2 provisioning/startup timeout after correcting an invalid URL, architecture mismatch, and expiring Instance Connect polling.
